__fixture__.name: future_extension_overflow_decision
__fixture__.scenario: An extension-contributed dependency view requests a primary rail entry and private sidebar. The reviewer denies primary placement because the seven-rank budget is already allocated, routes the view through Extensions plus rail overflow, and requires command-backed access rather than a duplicate global sidebar.

candidate_summary: Extension-contributed dependency explorer with durable package graph rows, provider status, and open-target actions.
decision_factors: The primary rail budget is already allocated to seven section ranks.
Dependency inventory is structurally adjacent to extension/package ownership and can attach under Extensions or overflow without creating a second sidebar.
The extension must expose a command-palette route and cannot rely on a private rail-only affordance.
Any install, update, remove, trust, or publisher action from the view requires its own command descriptor and review posture.

required_command_bindings.notes: The dependency view may be reached through command palette and rail overflow handoff, not a new primary rail rank.

duplicate_surface_analysis: A new dependency primary rail entry would duplicate Extensions and fracture extension/package state. The conforming placement is an Extensions subsection plus overflow handoff, with all launch-bearing actions resolving through command descriptors.
